title: draba pilosa var. oreades
intro: Draba pilosa var. oreades, also known as alpine whitlowgrass, is a rare, annual or biennial plant belonging to the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its small white flowers and hairy leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Draba pilosa var. oreades include its potential to reduce inflammation, prevent infections, and possibly even alleviate symptoms of certain respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often combined with other herbs in traditional remedies.
